Neighborhood Guide · Comal County, TX

Canyon Lake, TX — your guide to living on the water in the Hill Country

Veteran-owned Weichert brokerage. Locally rooted. Active in Canyon Lake every week.

Browse Canyon Lake Homes Talk to an Agent

About Canyon Lake

Eighty miles of shoreline. A community that chose to be here.

Canyon Lake is not a suburb. It is not a bedroom community for San Antonio or Austin. It is a place people move to on purpose — and the market reflects that.

Clean, spring-fed water carved through limestone bluffs. Hill Country terrain defined by live oaks, cedar, and rolling views worth sitting with. A tight-knit community where you know the people at the hardware store and the post office by name.

Canyon Lake is an unincorporated community in Comal County, Texas, about 20 miles northwest of New Braunfels and roughly 45 minutes north of downtown San Antonio. Comal ISD serves the area, with Smithson Valley High School among the well-rated campuses. Despite the peaceful, remote feel, you're 15 to 20 minutes from New Braunfels shopping, dining, and medical facilities on I-35.

Gruene Historic District is a 15-minute drive. Downtown New Braunfels and Wurstfest are 20 minutes. San Antonio's museums, restaurants, and medical center are under an hour.

Ready to buy in Canyon Lake?

Our team is active in the Canyon Lake market every week. We know the neighborhoods, HOAs, and well and septic considerations that matter.

Browse Listings
(830) 632-5725 Send a message 133 N Seguin Ave, New Braunfels

Life on the Lake

The Canyon Lake lifestyle

The water runs the calendar here. Boating, fishing, kayaking, and swimming fill the warm months. The Guadalupe River below the dam is one of the best tubing and fly-fishing stretches in Texas. Several marinas offer boat storage, launch access, and rentals.

On land, it's live oaks, cedar, and limestone outcroppings — rolling hills that give most properties a view worth sitting with. Hiking trails, cycling routes, and horseback riding are part of the landscape. The community is tight and volunteer-driven — Canyon Lake Area Chamber of Commerce, local VFW posts, and neighborhood associations keep things connected.

Where to Live

Popular Canyon Lake neighborhoods

Mystic Shores

Established master-planned community. Gated entry, community pool, boat ramp access, and a range of home styles from starter-friendly to custom builds. Lots from standard residential to 1+ acre.

$350,000 – $600,000+

The Summit Estates at Fischer

North side of the lake. Larger lots (1 to 5+ acres), Hill Country views, and a quieter, more rural feel. Good fit for buyers who want space and privacy without being far from the water.

$400,000 – $800,000+

River Chase

Along the Guadalupe River below Canyon Dam. Waterfront and river-access living with community amenities including a pool, park, and walking trails.

$300,000 – $550,000

Canyon Lake Hills

The original residential area of Canyon Lake. Established homes on varied lot sizes, many with mature landscaping and direct lake access or views. A more affordable entry point for the area.

$275,000 – $450,000

Hancock

Quieter, unincorporated area on the east side of the lake. Less developed, more acreage-oriented, and popular with buyers who want rural living with lake proximity.

Varies by acreage

Veteran Community

Canyon Lake has a strong military presence

According to U.S. Census Bureau data, approximately 3,381 veterans call Canyon Lake home — one of the higher concentrations in Comal County. Many served during the Gulf War era, with notable Vietnam-era representation as well. The community actively supports its veteran population through two local VFW posts.

If you're a veteran or active-duty service member, a VA home loan may let you buy here with no down payment. Our team is veteran-owned and works with VA buyers regularly.

Local VFW Posts
VFW Post 8573
16425 River Road, Canyon Lake
VFW Post 8800 — Startz Memorial
7755 FM 2673, Startzville

~$400K

Before You Buy

What to know before buying in Canyon Lake

Water & Septic

Many properties are on well water and septic systems. Test the well during inspection for flow rate and water quality. Inspect the septic for age, capacity, and county compliance. Budget for ongoing maintenance.

Flood Zones

The Army Corps of Engineers manages lake levels. Properties near the shoreline may fall within FEMA flood zones. Always check flood zone status before making an offer. Flood insurance can add $1,500 to $3,000 or more per year.

HOA Considerations

Master-planned communities like Mystic Shores and River Chase have HOA fees and architectural guidelines. Unincorporated areas generally do not. Know which you're buying into — especially for short-term rental intentions.

Wildlife & Land

Canyon Lake properties regularly share space with white-tailed deer, wild turkeys, and occasionally feral hogs. For buyers with acreage, wildlife management exemptions can provide significant property tax savings.

Common Questions

Frequently asked questions

Is Canyon Lake a good place to live year-round?

Yes. While the area draws visitors in the summer, Canyon Lake is a year-round residential community with schools, churches, medical facilities, and a full-time population of approximately 25,000 in the surrounding area.

How far is Canyon Lake from San Antonio?

Roughly 45 minutes to downtown San Antonio via US-281 or I-35 through New Braunfels. JBSA Randolph is approximately 40 minutes south.

Are there waterfront homes available under $500,000?

Lake-view and lake-access homes can be found under $500,000. True lakefront properties with dock or direct shoreline access typically start in the $500,000 to $600,000 range, though occasional opportunities appear below that threshold.

What school district serves Canyon Lake?

Comal ISD. Students attend schools including Smithson Valley High School, Spring Branch Middle School, and several well-rated elementary campuses.

Does Weichert Corwin serve the Canyon Lake area?

Yes. Canyon Lake is one of our primary service areas. Our agents know the neighborhoods, the HOAs, the well and septic considerations, and the lake-specific factors that matter when buying here. Our buyer rebate program applies to all Canyon Lake purchases.
